stem: An engineer takes 20 measurements of tire pressure using a pressure gauge. The measurements have a mean of 170.2 psi and a standard deviation of 13.0 psi. However, calibration tests reveal that a pressure gauge systematically overestimates the true value by between 1.7% and 4.4%. Estimate the true tire pressure and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 170.2 psi. After correcting for the 3.1% bias: ≈ 165.1 psi. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (1.7%-4.4%) and sampling variability: [157.2, 173.2]. Point estimate: 165.1 psi.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
